chapter_title.en: Chapter 99 – Disciplines of the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/332)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/332)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلَيْنِ اقْتَتَلَا وَ هُمَا مُحْرِمَانِ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ بِئْسَ مَا صَنَعَا قُلْتُ قَدْ فَعَلَا فَمَا الَّذِي يَلْزَمُهُمَا قَالَ عَلَى كُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا دَمٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Hafs Bin Bakhtary, from Abu Hallal Al Razy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the two men (pushing and) pulling each other, and they were both in Ihraam. Heasws said: ‘Glory be to Allahazwj! Evil is what they have both done’. I said, ‘They have done it. So what is that which necessitates the two of them?’ Heasws said: ‘Upon each one of them is blood (of a sacrificial animal)’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy from abu Halal al-Raziy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of two men who fought each other while in the state of Ihram. He (the Imam) said, ‘Allah is free of all defects, how bad is what they have done!’ I said, ‘They have done it but what must they do?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘They must each offer one animal as offering.’”
